findings.description: The source describes two workarounds that exist before the `copyBeanToRequestScopeOnError` solution: (1) using `<n:set>` to store request parameter in a variable, and (2) using implicit object `param`. The knowledge file omits these workarounds entirely, including the JSP code example showing the `<n:set>` approach. Users who cannot change the component configuration would need these alternatives.
findings.source_evidence:
* 一度Nablarchタグ ``<n:set>`` を使用してリクエストパラメータの値を変数に格納する
* 暗黙オブジェクト ``param`` を使用してリクエストパラメータにアクセスする

前者の ``<n:set>`` を使用する例:
<n:set var="quantity" name="form.quantity" />
<c:if test="${quantity >= 100}">

findings.description: `@Domain` annotation appears in the code example (`@Domain("name")`) but is absent from the section hints.
`HttpResponse` and `ExecutionContext` class names appear in the code example but are not listed in the section hints.
findings.source_evidence: public HttpResponse create(HttpRequest request, ExecutionContext context) {
